page: Vultr voc-g-32c-128gb-640s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 32 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. The system is powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 physical cores with a single thread per core, operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared. Storage is provided via a bundled 640 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance in single-core CPU stress tests and large-block memory bandwidth, both reading and writing, placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core CPU performance, bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, and PassMark CPU metrics (including encryption and physics tests) fall into the strong category, representing the top 25%. Redis SET operations also demonstrate strong performance, executing 73.66 million operations per second. Conversely, static web serving throughput and database operations register in the average tier.

This server offers a balanced resource profile with a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring high memory capacity and core density without the premium of dedicated CPU threads. However, the shared CPU allocation introduces potential performance variability under sustained, concurrent multi-core loads. This configuration is suitable for multi-threaded decompression tasks, Redis caching layers, intermediate-scale database operations, and general-purpose workloads that benefit from fast local NVMe storage.
description: Vultr voc-g-32c-128gb-640s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 32 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor on the x86_64 architecture. Operating at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation, it includes a bundled 640 GB NVMe SSD. Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in single-core CPU stress tests and large-block memory bandwidth, alongside strong multi-core CPU and Redis SET performance. However, static web serving and database operations perform in the average tier. The shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff for sustained workloads, but the high resource density offers qualitative cost efficiency. This server is suitable for multi-threaded decompression, Redis caching, and general-purpose applications requiring substantial memory and fast local storage.

tagline: A balanced x86_64 server featuring thirty-two AMD EPYC cores, high memory bandwidth, and integrated NVMe storage for general-purpose workloads.

128.0 GB RAM providing 4.0 GB per core
Bundled 640 GB NVMe SSD local storage
